domain: Trobriand kula ring (Massim 18-island ceremonial exchange; soulava red-shell necklaces CW + mwali white-shell armbands CCW; opposing-handedness paired flows; parity violation = ritual breach)
specific_mechanism: Paired forward+backward attention with parity-conservation per token-pair; sum of sign-of-difference signatures over context should be constant; deviation flags topological defect (hallucination).
llm_application: KULA-PARITY: a topological-defect detector for hallucination via paired bidirectional attention. Each layer maintains two attention modes — FORWARD (standard causal) and BACKWARD (acausal sliding-window). For each token pair (i,j) the parity signature s_{ij} = sign(attn_fwd[i,j] - attn_bwd[j,i]); the SUM Σ_{i,j} s_{ij} should be conserved (KULA conservation law) for grounded responses but DEVIATE for hallucinated responses. Deviation magnitude = topological defect score. Differs from TOHA Topological Divergence 2504.10063 (single-direction attention graph), bidirectional BERT (no parity signature), Self-Consistency (output-level not attention) by combining (a) PAIRED forward+backward attention, (b) PARITY-CONSERVATION law, (c) topological-defect scoring.
novelty_claim: TOHA + bidirectional attention literature + Topological Data Analysis already exist. KULA-PARITY recombines paired-direction + conservation-law. Small extension.
